Turn 48 ‚ÄĒ Certificate D.4: coefficient d=8 quotient witness I used only the files attached to this turn. The standing problem remains the over-G isomorphism question for őďS,őďH, and the sweep is now in the reproducibility/certificate phase rather than the obstruction-search phase. The previous turn left exactly one remaining nonlinear certificate block after the A2,d=4 scalar package: the coefficient d=8 quotient witnessőł80(0,B8)=(B8)[2].The history identifies this as the final coefficient d=8 witness needed to show that the residual codimension-one miss disappears after quotienting by imőł80.Certificate D.4: the d=8 quotient witness

Work in the residual Ef, mod-2, s=1 Magnus setting with

X=1+A,Y=1+B,and with free restricted Lie algebra L‚ąô‚äāF2‚ü®‚ü®A,B‚ü©‚ü©.

The coefficient A2=R/(4,t) secondary obstruction group in root degree d isHd(2)=Ld[2]/imőłd0,whereőłd0:ker(Ld‚äēLd[A,‚ąí]+[B,‚ąí]Ld+1)‚ü∂Ld[2],(u,v)‚Ü¶u[2]+v[2].This is the same secondary quotient isolated in the prior coefficient audit; the history records that d=8 was the exceptional-looking case because the residual high torsor had a codimension-one miss in L8[2].Letb8:=B[8]=B8‚ąąL8.

In characteristic 2,[B,b8]=BB8+B8B=B9+B9=0.

Therefore(0,b8)‚ąąker(L8‚äēL8[A,‚ąí]+[B,‚ąí]L9).

Applying őł80 givesőł80(0,b8)=0[2]+b8[2]=(B8)[2]=B16.Now recall the residual degree-16 high-torsor picture. InV:=L8[2],the residual high-correction image is the hyperplane

M=kerőĽ,where őĽ=coeff ((AB+BA)4)[2]+coeff(B8)[2].The history records exactly this codimension-one high image and this missing functional.ButőĽ((B8)[2])=1.Thus(B8)[2]‚ąą/M.

Since M is a hyperplane in V=L8[2], and imőł80 contains a vector outside M, we get

M+imőł80=V.

Passing to the coefficient secondary quotientH8(2)=V/imőł80,the image of M is therefore all of H8(2). Equivalently,B8‚Ü†H8(2).

So the d=8 first-A2 secondary coefficient obstruction dies without needing a fixed-tail computation. The residual high torsor misses exactly the őĽ-line, but that missing line is killed by the zero-lower-ghost secondary relation represented by (0,B8). This is precisely the uniform pattern requested: rather than hammering an actual H8(2)-tail, we identify the structural quotient that annihilates the only residual exceptional direction.Appendix-ready D.4 verification D.4 reduces to four checkable lines.

Define b8=B8‚ąąL8.

Verify the kernel condition:[A,0]+[B,b8]=0.

Apply the secondary map:őł80(0,b8)=b8[2]=(B8)[2].

Evaluate the residual missing functional:őĽ((B8)[2])=1.

Therefore imőł80ÓÄ†‚äÜkerőĽ, so the residual hyperplane image becomes surjective after quotienting:kerőĽ‚ü∂L8[2]/imőł80 is onto.This completes Certificate D.4.

Status after Turn 48

The nonlinear certificate ledger now stands as follows:

The only remaining reproducibility item is not mathematical: D.3 still lacks the raw coordinate vector of one őļ‚ąąkerD5,6,7 whose coefficient-Bockstein shadow changes the d=4 square coordinates by (1,1,0,0,0,0).The history already records the existence and scalar effect of such a őļ, but not its coordinates in the chosen 3L5‚äē3L6‚äē3L7 basis.

ESTABLISHED: Certificate D.4 is explicit. Since (0,B8)‚ąąker([A,‚ąí]+[B,‚ąí]), őł80(0,B8)=(B8)[2]. This class has őĽ=1, so imőł80 is not contained in the residual high hyperplane kerőĽ. Hence the residual codimension-one miss vanishes in H8(2), and B8‚Ü†H8(2).

GAP: No structural obstruction remains. The remaining reproducibility gap is only the raw coordinate printout for the D.3 continuation-kernel vector őļ‚ąąkerD5,6,7.

NEXT: Produce the missing D.3 coordinate certificate: choose the fixed 3L5‚äē3L6‚äē3L7 basis, list one explicit őļ, verify D5,6,7 őļ=0, and verify that its coefficient-Bockstein shadow is (1,1,0,0,0,0).[TURN48-CERT-D4-D8-QUOTIENT-WITNESS]
